name: Southwestern Chicken Filo Triangles
recipeIngredient: 2 whole skinless boneless chicken breasts, cooked, shredded and chopped
1 red bell pepper, seeded and finely chopped
4 Roma tomatoes, peeled, seeded and finely chopped
16 ounces phyllo dough, thawed in refrigerator
1 cup butter, or as needed, melted
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the thawed phyllo dough crosswise into 5 equal sections. If serving as an entree, cut into 4 sections for larger triangles.
2. In a medium bowl, combine chicken, cheese, red bell pepper, Roma tomatoes, green chilies, and cilantro. Mix well.
3. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
4. For each section of phyllo dough (cover others with a slightly damp cloth), lay pieces flat on a large surface. Brush all sides with melted butter. Turn over and brush the other side.
5. Place a teaspoon of chicken mixture at one end of each piece. Fold the bottom left corner over the filling, aligning the bottom edge with the side edge. Continue folding in this manner until a multi-folded triangle is formed. Keep triangles under a damp cloth.
6. On an oiled baking sheet, place filo triangles and bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
7. Serve triangles with Corn Relish and Guacamole on the side.
RELISH:
In a medium large sauté pan, heat olive oil over medium until hot. Add remaining ingredients and cook for 10 minutes or until corn is done. Makes approximately 4 cups.
